author: @samosdolphins123
text: professor, isn't alcl3 reactive with moisture/water to give hcl toxic gas?  how to avoid this?

author: @n1mbusmusic606
text: Rob I have a question. James lovelock in "novacene" and other books(big fan of his) says that melting down plastics and turning to fuel our best bet(or repurposing like prescious plastic or rice university's flash graphene tech) because it doesn't release methane like when burning biomass. Ppl say it's too energy intensive, meaning too much heat required to melt down plastic for fuel conversion, but don't deep eutectic solvents lower the melting of mixtures significantly? Or are the other steps in the fuel conversion process super energy intensive?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: sorry mate - don't know

author: @bloodyricho1
text: I am a sailor I have a yacht andI can see so many different ways to uses this to generate power in a yachting situation. With this method you could have a soft diaphragm on two sides of a cell. Mount this  on the stern area of the yacht and the see sawing action of the boat will cause an up down effect on electrolyte. The original Swag cell mounted inline with the hot water outlet on the vessels engine would be very efficient. An open cell mounted on the stern with horizontal and vertical or at 45 to the horizontal would average a 6 knot flow plus the see saw speeds of the water. Unreal idea for yatchies every where. Great work Robert! I am excited and so are the yatchies I have spoken to.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: awesome mate - i haven't done anything on this for a while as i have been concentrating on other stuff for a bit but i will get back to it soon

author: @farazsayed5730
text: It's possible that the two different aligned graphene lines have different affinities to the two ions in the solution. Could you try pull out the different alignments to the different electrodes when making a new SWAG Generator and run some more experiments to see the change. Otherwise scrape between the row and electrode to disconnect each type from the other electrode. If you're still thinking of dropping the salt down the generator, I imagine the size of the droplet will have and effect on the reading you get. Thanks for making the video

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good ideas mate - I will give it a go

author: @johnwagner7853
text: Plant pot or pot plant.  If it's the latter, it explains her understanding :)  Keep up the great work!

author: @fabrizioricciarelli
text:
Really impressive...every time you publish a video I get shocked and happy. 

As you already know, I'm starting experimenting with lasers, in different wavelenghts, projected onto the surfaces prepared as explained in your videos: I've only to find a way to scatter the beam and diffuse it all over the surface...mmmmhhh...perhaps a motorized prism could do the work.

When do you think to sell, inside of your webshop, all the components needed to produce a solar cell of yours? A ready-to-use product would be ideal...like the GO paint.

Great work, love you!

author: @bobdring
text: Great work. You really need to get an extra meter. There may be a substantial error computing watts using short circuit current multiplied by open circuit voltage.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: will do

replies.author: @sceptic33
replies.text: my favourite trick is to put an oscilloscope across a 1ohm resistor in the circuit so the V = A and you can see them together

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
sceptic33
don't have an oscilloscope

author: @jfausset
text: I think you mean 240 W per square meter, not per square centimeter. This is great progress. I hope the experiment bears out as you hypothesize.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yeah i did - but when i reviewed it i realised i had correctly linked it to solar cells - so i thought ah well people will know what i mean - so i didn't do it again

author: @VAXKILLEDMYDAD
text: Great progress!  I'm not sure I followed exactly what you were doing with the darker and lighter lines caused by the magnetic field. Were you scraping the darker lines, or the lighter spaces in between? And you used a pin? Did you put the copper strips separately on the light and dark areas? Hope you have a nice time in Sweden

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i scraped away the interface between the light and dark and attached an electrode to each area - so one for the dark area and one for the light area - it is cold here lol
